title: guzmania lingulata var. cardinalis
intro: Guzmania lingulata var. cardinalis, also known as Cardinal's Guzmania, is a variety of the Guzmania lingulata plant, a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is valued for its vibrant red inflorescence, which can add a pop of color to any room. The plant is also used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, and has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. Guzmania lingulata var. cardinalis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has been reported to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and aiding in the recovery from respiratory infections.
